frameworks.name: attractor neural networks
frameworks.one_line: delay activity sustained by stable fixed-point attractors with constant rates

frameworks.name: chaotic reservoir networks
frameworks.one_line: delay activity arises from high-dimensional chaotic recurrent dynamics

frameworks.name: recurrent neural networks trained with backpropagation through time
frameworks.one_line: task-optimized RNNs produce delay trajectories shaped by training objective

frameworks.name: low-dimensional neural trajectory analysis
frameworks.one_line: characterize delay dynamics via cumulative dimensionality and time decodability

core_question: How is information held during delays, and what mechanisms encode the passage of time across tasks?
key_assumptions: delay-period neural activity supports working memory and time estimation
candidate network classes make distinguishable predictions about delay dynamics
passage of time can be read out from population activity
manipulations: task relevance of timing information across four primate datasets
comparison across three classes of recurrent network models
analysis method: time decoding vs cumulative trajectory dimensionality
key_findings: time is decoded with high precision when timing is task-relevant
time is decoded with lower precision when timing is task-irrelevant
cumulative dimensionality of delay trajectories is low across all datasets
interpretations: working memory need not rely on constant rates around a fixed attractor pattern
low-dimensional trajectories constrain plausible mechanisms of time encoding
